Did You Know…?
The New Forest Inn sits in the tiny hamlet of Emery Down, right in the heart of the
historic New Forest — an area once created by William the Conqueror in 1079 as a
royal hunting ground. Today, the Forest’s famous free-roaming ponies still wander
the roads and commons, just as they have for centuries.

Who Are Our Burgers…?
Commoners of the Forest are Those Who Occupy Land or Property Where
Privileges are attached, including the Right to Graze Stock on The Open Forest!
Verderers are Forestry Officials in England Who Deal with Common Land in
Certain Formal Royal Hunting Areas Which Are Property of The Crown!
Commissioners are Government Officials Responsible for Protecting, Expanding
& Promoting Sustainable Management of Forest Land!
Agisters are officials employed by the Verderers of the New Forest to manage
the welfare of free-roaming livestock. They monitor ponies, cattle, donkeys,
pigs, and sheep, ensuring animals are healthy and byelaws are followed.
Rangers are responsible for helping protect, manage, and care for the New
Forest, the wildlife, and the people who visit here.

NFI PERFECT PIES
"Our pies are made fresh daily with love by our talented chefs. Locals
and visitors alike come from near and far to enjoy the flavours of the
New Forest Inn — hearty, wholesome, and simply unforgettable."
‘The Rufus Stone Pie’
A Hearty Classic, Fit for a King!!
Traditional Steak & Ale £20.99
Served with Chips, Seasonal Vegetables and Gravy
“Pairs Perfectly with a glass of our Argentinian Norton Malbec”
‘The Lyndhurst Pie’
Elegant and Full of Local Flavour, a nod to the Capital of The New Forest
Pulled Chicken, Leek and Tarragon Pie £20.99
Served with Chips, Seasonal Vegetables and Gravy
“Pairs Perfectly with a glass of our New Zealand Feather Drop Sauvignon Blanc”
‘The Lymington Pie’
Celebrating the charming New Forest coastal town of Lymington — fresh, local, and a splash of spicy
seaside flavour
Curried Smoked Haddock Pie
GF
£20.99
Topped with Mash, and Served with Butter Glazed Greens
“Pairs Perfectly with a glass of our South African Neil Joubert Chenin Blanc”
‘The Beaulieu Abbey Pie’
Inspired by The Historic Abbey founded in 1203, Rich, Layered and Full of Character
Slow Cooked Mexican Pulled Pork and Mexicana Cheddar Pie £20.99
Served with Sour Cream and Chive Crushed Potatoes, BBQ Beans and Tomato Salsa
“Pairs Perfectly with a glass of our Italian Visconti Della Rocca Primitivo”
‘The Bolderwood Pie’
Named after the Beautiful Bolderwood area in The New Forest, Elegant,
Green and Comforting
Creamy Camembert, Thyme & Wild MushroomV £19.99
Served with Garlic and Herb Buttered New Potatoes, Spring Vegetables and Gravy
“Pairs Perfectly with a glass of our Chilean Santa Alegra Chardonnay or our Spanish
Morande Pionero Pinot Noir Reserva”
ALL PIES, EXCEPT OUR FISH PIE ARE SERVED AS A SLICE OF “PROPER” PIE WITH A SHORTCRUST PASTRY ENCASING!
